mandate: Define how each partner type uses WedFlow. Output to docs/partner-journey.md.
keyFindings: The officiant channel is the only validated partner type. 12 couples from one officiant (James Toonk) is strong signal. Church, counsellor, and vendor are designed but unproven.

The partner dashboard was built after the first 12 referrals happened. The dashboard is for retention and scale, not for triggering first referrals. Partners refer through conversation, not through software.
Revenue share is the biggest gap for partner motivation beyond goodwill. No Stripe Connect, no commission engine, no payout mechanism exists.
Church is the scalable unit. One church with multiple officiants multiplies referral surface without proportional founder effort. The parent_partner_id hierarchy supports this.
Counsellors and vendors have distinct referral triggers (emotional vs logistical) that should inform the pitch, even though the dashboard is identical for all types.
whatWorked: Reading the partner agent testaments gave a complete picture of what was built without needing to trace through every component file. The semantic memory consolidated files provided the Base pilot context efficiently.
recommendationsForNextAgent: When building partner-facing materials (one-pagers, pitch decks, onboarding guides), use the journey map as the source of truth for what each partner type cares about. The vendor journey is the least defined and should not be invested in until officiant/church is proven at 5+ partners.
spiritContribution: Documented the B2B2C partner model grounded in what the product actually does today, with honest gap analysis. This prevents future agents from building aspirational features that skip over unresolved basics like revenue share and partner onboarding.
